item.title: ‘I’m a loner’: I’m 62 with a multimillion-dollar portfolio and no heirs. Should I find a wife for my twilight years?
item.description: “I have never been married, have no siblings, and both my parents have passed away.”

item.title: ‘I feel the clock ticking’: My wife and I are in our 60s — and employ 48 people in a small town. Can we ever retire?
item.description: “We could survive financially, but my people would not.”

item.title: ‘It’s a life-changing sum’: Can I stop my kids from using their inheritance to support political causes I vehemently oppose?
item.description: ”I would hate for our money to end up in the hands of organizations that oppose everything we have supported throughout our lives.”

item.title: ‘I’m close to retiring’: I’m behind on my property tax. Is this a really bad time to sell my house?
item.description: “I’ve never been late on a mortgage, so this makes me nervous.”

item.title: ‘Am I crazy?’ My boyfriend’s parents say he doesn’t earn enough to marry me. Who is to blame here?
item.description: The Moneyist: “I have a Valentine’s message for you, but you won’t read it in a Hallmark card.”
